hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W, F, Y) will enhance complementarity with SSTR2-unique ECL2 residues (e.g., 192, 193), while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L, I, V) will reduce off-target interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5 by avoiding off-target-conserved polar interactions. This will increase the selectivity margin (Δmargin) while maintaining strong SSTR2 binding.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current designs are achieving good selectivity (Δmargin > 0), but structural issues are limiting the number of viable candidates. Tightening structural constraints and focusing on disruption of off-target interactions at non-pharmacophore positions should improve both structural quality and selectivity.

proposed_changes.rationale: Reducing the allowed clash score will enforce better structural quality and reduce steric clashes that may be causing structural failures.

Focusing optimization on non-pharmacophore positions known to contribute to off-target binding (SSTR3/SSTR5 conserved-pocket contacts) will help disrupt off-target interactions while preserving key pharmacophore residues (FWKT at 7-10 and Cys3/Cys14).
